You’ll need to define the characteristics you’re looking for in your acquisition target.
Otherwise, you’ll have no parameters to narrow your search.
Here’s a list of questions to go through to start to form your deal requirements…
How much do you want to work in the business once you acquire it?
________________________________________________________
________________________________________________________
Do you want to keep your full-time job after acquiring this business?
• 	Yes
• 	No
Do you want to manage other people?
• 	Yes
• 	No
Have you operated a business where someone else manages your team before?
• 	Yes
• 	No
Do you want to be heavily involved and oversee a lot of the day-to-day details of
the business?
• 	Yes
• 	No
Do you want to spend all of your time setting the vision and finding new
talent/new projects for the business?
Deal Requirement Questionnaire

• 	Less cash-flow than the amount you want to earn year one
• 	Working in the business
• 	Not having to manage others
• 	Freedom to spend 100% of time setting the vision
• 	Acquiring a business that’s outside of your skill set
• 	Picking an industry you’re passionate about
Which parameters ARE negotiable if you needed to choose between imperfect
business leads?
• 	Doing Work You Hate
• 	Having the business farther away than your ideal distance
Less cash-flow than the amount you want to earn year one
• 	Working in the business
• 	Not having to manage others
• 	Freedom to spend 100% of time setting the vision
• 	Acquiring a business that’s outside of your skill set
• 	Picking an industry you’re passionate about
After you complete the assignment, you need to go back and read through your
answers and look for contradictions… evaluate if you’ve made your parameters TOO
strict (if you’re unsure, then stick with what you’ve got, and if you’re later
generating no leads, there’s a good chance your parameters are too strict) or made
your answers or parameters too unrealistic.
For example, if you said you want to work in the businesses day to day… but then
also said you want to focus 100% of your time setting the vision and letting
someone else manage the business, that doesn’t make any sense. You need to think
critically about that and reanswer in a consistent way.
Or, as another example, if you said you want a business that’s going to cash-flow
you $5 million a year one, know that you’re very likely being too restrictive. It’s
going to be harder to find and close a business that can provide you that much cash
in the first year.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 9 --

If you’ve said you don’t want to manage the business… you want to check in only
once per week… and you want multiple six figures in income from the business… yet
you have no professional experience whatsoever. You’re being too unrealistic.
Review all of your answers as you're done, and use good judgment for whether
you’re being too strict, contradictory and need to think more, or too unrealistic for
your situation and experience-level. Adjust accordingly, because you’ll be using this
to target the right business and later, evaluate them,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
